sections.paragraph:
(Cogn. roots כָּאָה and קָהָה) fut. וַיֵּכַהּ.-

(1) to be feeble, to fail in strength, to be cast down in mind, Isa 42:4. Specially,

(2) used of a lamp about to go out (see adj. Isa 42:3); of eyes become dim, whether by age, Deu 34:7 Zec 11:17 Gen 27:1 or by grief, Job 17:7.

Piel כִּהָה and כֵּהָה-

(1) intrans. to becomepale, as a spot on the skin, Levit. 13:6 , 21 Lev 13:21, 26 Lev 13:26, 28 Lev 13:28, 56 Lev 13:56also to be feeble, timid, to be cast down in mind, Eze 21:12 comp. Isa 61:3.

(2) to chide, to restrain any one; 1Sa 3:13, וְלֹא כִהָה בָּם “and (that) he did not chide them;” i.e. restrain them. Compare כָּאָה. Hence-
